title: GMS Week 26 - TRAFFIC DOUBLES, YARDS WAIT
section_path: GMS Week 26 - TRAFFIC DOUBLES, YARDS WAIT
section_path_text: GMS Week 26 - TRAFFIC DOUBLES, YARDS WAIT
summary:
Main
The war is over; the paperwork is taking longer than the peace. The formal signing of the US-Iranagreement at Bürgenstock in Switzerland was abruptly postponed last Friday, with Vice President Vance turning his plane around over what the White House called unresolved logistical issues, even as the interim memorandum remained in effect and implementation accelerated on the water. Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz doubled in 24 hours to its highest level since late February, with at least 34 ships exiting into the Gulf of Oman against a pre-war daily average of 110.
